risks.risk: TableMetadata Concurrency: Parallel commits conflict during rest metastore swaps.
risks.mitigation: Utilize optimistic retry policies and enforce transaction stubs with clean snapshot-isolation rollback boundaries.

risks.risk: Serialization Breakages: Introducing properties or spec version gating breaks minor engine API schemas.
risks.mitigation: Enforce custom kebab-case parsers (e.g. TableMetadataParser) without using Jackson annotations; register revapi track gates.

risks.risk: Resource Leaks: Large manifest-group iterations mask I/O leaks in streaming pipelines.
risks.mitigation: Always wrap closeable collections in D3-like custom try-with-resources stubs using Iceberg's CloseableIterable wrappers.

openQuestions.question: Do we allow editing deletes, or only soft deletes?
openQuestions.details: Soft deletes are simpler to persist in V2 format lists, whereas complete rewrites during compaction require sequence number gating.
openQuestions.owner: Decide with · Community Consensus, before Milestone 3

openQuestions.question: Vectorized offset cache sizes inside pyiceberg
openQuestions.details: Setting negative limits disables caching. We should confirm standard memory bounds across all client catalogs to avoid memory leakage.
openQuestions.owner: Decide with · binding maintainers, before Milestone 4

0.title: Metadata Snapshot Isolation
0.desc: Establishes base table metadata swap isolation boundaries, tracking snapshot lists and transaction state swaps atomically.

tasks.text: Implement serializable check-and-put swap metastore validation

1.title: Retry Commit Hook
1.desc: Implements dynamic commit-retry logic pushed in by Iceberg, allowing transactional retries when conflicts occur during catalog metadata swaps.

tasks.text: Support transactional retry policies and exponential backoffs
Write transactional metastore conflict resolver unit tests

2.title: Atomic Replace API
2.desc: Exposes powerful, atomic replace endpoints inside transaction boundaries to replace file pointers or schemas atomically.

tasks.text: Expose atomic replace API interface methods
Integrate catalog check-and-put swap payload generation

3.title: Layout Compaction & Rewrite
3.desc: Dynamic background compactor that merges small data and deletes files. Integrates with the Replace API to swap layout metadata atomically.

tasks.text: Code file layout compactors and partition bin-pack merges
Enforce delete file sequence number integrity checks during rewrites
Trigger orphan file garbage collection scans upon rewrite swaps

plan-1779509761391.name: PyIceberg Maintenance
plan-1779509761391.docTldr: This roadmap designs maintenance, metadata pruning, and optimization sweeps for the PyIceberg project. It outlines direct CLI sweeps for orphan files, transactional retry stubs during conflict metadata swaps, and memory-safe Bloom filters for scaling massive tables.
docNotes: Validate S3 direct list limitations: large directory listings trigger S3 throttling and represent high cost.
Enforce strict 24-hour grace periods to avoid deleting newly added concurrent files before manifest commits.
Implement DynamoDB or conditional S3 conditional check-and-put stubs for transactional catalog swaps.

risks.risk: Orphan File Cleanup Risk: Remove orphan files utility might delete newly added concurrent files before commit completes.
risks.mitigation: Validate file timestamps against a grace period (e.g., older than 24 hours) and cross-reference with active manifest lists before physical file purging.

risks.risk: Catalog Lock Availability: Lack of distributed transactions in standard file systems causing corrupt metadata swap states.
risks.mitigation: Implement catalog-specific locking mechanisms (like S3 conditional writes or DynamoDB locks) for metadata swapping.

risks.risk: High Memory Overhead during Orphan Scans: Scanning millions of files in large catalogs raises OOM crashes.
risks.mitigation: Leverage streaming directory iterators and perform in-memory filter checks using space-efficient Bloom filters or partitioned hash sets.

openQuestions.question: Should we provide an interactive CLI review step?
openQuestions.details: Deleting files is destructive. Providing a preview option showing dry-run output prevents catastrophic human error.
openQuestions.owner: Decide with · Maintainer Review, before Milestone 3

openQuestions.question: Support AWS S3 inventory scans instead of direct listing?
openQuestions.details: S3 direct directory listing is extremely slow and expensive for massive tables. Inventory files would scale beautifully.
openQuestions.owner: Decide with · AWS Catalog Parity, before Milestone 4

plan-1779594189720.name: PyIceberg MoR
plan-1779594189720.docTldr: This roadmap outlines high-performance Merge-on-Read (MoR) vectorized reads inside the python bindings. It implements position delete bitmap lookups, lazily mapped segment-level RoaringPositionBitmaps, and PyArrow vectorized filter engines on-the-fly.
docNotes: Load position deletes lazily once per manifest group to avoid massive overhead inside parallel scan loops.
Enforce vectorized binary search indices inside PyArrow RecordBatch columns for fast deletion vector masking.
Restrict first release scope strictly to reading V2 format specs, leaving MoR write pipelines for subsequent cycles.

risks.risk: Delete Filter Latency in Scans: Applying position deletes on-the-fly inside table scan iterators drastically degrades throughput.
risks.mitigation: Load position delete bitmaps once per manifest group and run vectorized binary search filtering on PyArrow RecordBatch columns.

risks.risk: Roaring Bitmap Memory Explosion: Large position delete bitmaps consume excessive heap memory.
risks.mitigation: Implement segment-level RoaringPositionBitmap lazy-loading, serializing chunks to direct binary buffers rather than mapping python integers.

risks.risk: Equality Delete Evaluation: Dynamically matching row values across custom partition bounds is CPU intensive.
risks.mitigation: Restrict equality deletes to specific primary key columns and pre-compile projection index maps inside the scan planner.

openQuestions.question: Do we support writing equality deletes, or only reading them?
openQuestions.details: PyIceberg primarily focuses on analytical scan reads. Restricting MoR writes to position deletes first simplifies format v2 compliance.
openQuestions.owner: Decide with · Python community, before Milestone 1

openQuestions.question: Should delete files follow format v3 specifications?
openQuestions.details: Spec v3 introduces roaring bitmap format additions. Keeping v2 compatibility for position deletes guarantees engine interoperability.
openQuestions.owner: Decide with · Spec Parity, before Milestone 2
